questions.question:
Given that a person is a woman, what is the conditional relative frequency that she prefers Action? Round to the nearest percent.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Comedy} & \text{Action} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Men} & 30 & 50 & 80 \\ \hline \text{Women} & 40 & 20 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 70 & 70 & 140 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Women who prefer Action = 20. Total women = 60. Conditional relative frequency = 20/60 × 100 ≈ 33.3%, rounds to 33%.

questions.question:
Given that a student prefers English, what is the conditional relative frequency that the student is a boy? Round to the nearest percent.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Math} & \text{English} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Boys} & 45 & 15 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Girls} & 30 & 30 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 75 & 45 & 120 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Boys who prefer English = 15. Total who prefer English = 45. Conditional relative frequency = 15/45 × 100 ≈ 33.3%, rounds to 33%.

questions.question:
What is the joint relative frequency of owning both a dog and a cat? Express as a simplified fraction.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Dog} & \text{Cat} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Yes} & 40 & 20 & 60 \\ \hline \text{No} & 10 & 30 & 40 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 50 & 50 & 100 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Joint frequency of Dog AND Cat = 20. Grand total = 100. Joint relative frequency = 20/100 = 1/5.

questions.question:
Given that a student is a Junior, what percentage prefer Soccer?

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Basketball} & \text{Soccer} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Seniors} & 28 & 12 & 40 \\ \hline \text{Juniors} & 16 & 24 & 40 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 44 & 36 & 80 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Juniors who prefer Soccer = 24. Total Juniors = 40. Percentage = 24/40 × 100 = 60%.

questions.question:
What is the conditional relative frequency of answering 'Yes' given the student is in 10th grade? Express as a simplified fraction.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Yes} & \text{No} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{10th} & 36 & 24 & 60 \\ \hline \text{11th} & 18 & 42 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 54 & 66 & 120 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: 10th grade Yes = 36. Total 10th graders = 60. Conditional relative frequency = 36/60 = 3/5.

questions.question:
Given that a student is in Art, what is the conditional relative frequency that the student is a girl? Round to the nearest percent.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Art} & \text{Music} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Boys} & 25 & 35 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Girls} & 40 & 20 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 65 & 55 & 120 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Girls in Art = 40. Total in Art = 65. Conditional relative frequency = 40/65 × 100 ≈ 61.5%, rounds to 62%.

questions.question:
What is the joint relative frequency of being an 8th grader who rides the bus? Express as a percent, rounded to the nearest percent.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Car} & \text{Bus} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{7th} & 30 & 50 & 80 \\ \hline \text{8th} & 45 & 25 & 70 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 75 & 75 & 150 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: 8th graders who ride the bus = 25. Grand total = 150. Joint relative frequency = 25/150 × 100 ≈ 16.7%, rounds to 17%.

questions.question:
Given that a student prefers chips, what percentage are boys? Round to the nearest percent.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Chips} & \text{Fruit} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Boys} & 42 & 18 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Girls} & 24 & 36 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 66 & 54 & 120 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Boys who prefer chips = 42. Total who prefer chips = 66. Percentage = 42/66 × 100 ≈ 63.6%, rounds to 64%.

questions.question:
Given that a student is female, what fraction participates in Choir? Express in simplest form.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Drama} & \text{Choir} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Male} & 20 & 40 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Female} & 50 & 10 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 70 & 50 & 120 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Females in Choir = 10. Total females = 60. Fraction = 10/60 = 1/6.

questions.question:
Given that a student bikes to school, what is the conditional relative frequency that the student is in 9th grade? Round to the nearest percent.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Walk} & \text{Bike} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{9th} & 15 & 45 & 60 \\ \hline \text{10th} & 35 & 25 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 50 & 70 & 120 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: 9th graders who bike = 45. Total who bike = 70. Conditional relative frequency = 45/70 × 100 ≈ 64.3%, rounds to 64%.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You can accurately calculate conditional relative frequencies, joint relative frequencies, and interpret percentages from two-way tables.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Remember: conditional frequency means the denominator is a row total or column total, not the grand total. Check whether the question says 'given that' to identify the correct denominator.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Joint relative frequency uses the grand total as the denominator. Conditional relative frequency uses a row or column total. Read carefully to determine which one is needed.
scoreMessages.0: Start by: 1) Identifying the correct cell in the table, 2) Determining the denominator — grand total for joint, row/column total for conditional, 3) Dividing and converting to a fraction or percent as requested.
